title: Bubbia montana
taxonomy: Bubbia montana is classified in the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family is a part of the order Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants that are mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Annonaceae is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Bubbia montana belonging to one of these groups. The classification of Bubbia montana provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species. Understanding the classification of a plant like Bubbia montana is essential for its identification, conservation, and utilization.
